rationale: The citing paper uses Feinberg, Krishna, and Zhang (2002) to develop a specific behavioral mechanism in its theory section: consumers may react negatively when they see others getting better deals (betrayal/fairness), which directly motivates part of H3. Later, it cites the same work again to interpret observed adverse reactions when bundle requirements seem too stringent. This is more than background listing because the cited study informs both hypothesis construction and result interpretation for a key mechanism.
relation_description: Foubert and Gijsbrechts transfer the betrayal/fairness response from targeted-promotion contexts to bundle promotions, using it to explain why nonqualifying shoppers may switch away or reduce purchase incidence. The cited paper provides the behavioral mechanism that is applied in this new promotional setting.
